prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a graphic title sequence. Against a black background, large red block letters with white outlines read “INITIALIZE SEQUENCE.” Across the first several frames, the same wording remains centered while the background alternates between darker black and a gray glow or spotlight effect behind the text, creating a pulsing visual emphasis.

The scene then cuts to the interior of a retail computer store. A long display counter runs diagonally through the frame, holding desktop computer monitors, keyboards, mice, and tower cases. Shelving and stacked boxed products fill the left and rear areas. Fluorescent ceiling lights run in rows overhead. At the bottom of the frame, partially visible white text appears; it seems to include specifications such as “Celeron 400 MHz,” though the full line is cut off and unclear.

The next store view is wider and slightly blurred, showing more of the showroom. Computers and boxed items are arranged along both sides of an aisle, with additional displays and shelves extending toward the back of the store. The space appears densely stocked with computer equipment and accessories.

A promotional graphic then overlays a blurred store background. In the foreground are images of a desktop computer setup: tower cases, a monitor, speakers, a mouse, and boxed software or hardware, including a box labeled “CD-ROM.” Large layered text reads “FREE” in red at the top and “Internet Access” in yellow across the center. Small text near the bottom appears to say “See Store for Details,” though it is somewhat obscured.

The commercial then shifts to a blue graphic background with a green map of the United States. A horizontal banner at the top contains the “Computer Renaissance” name/logo, partly covered in this frame by large red pricing text reading “$999 99” or “999 99,” with the exact formatting difficult to distinguish.

The final frame keeps the blue map background and top “Computer Renaissance” banner. Centered white and yellow text provides store information: “250 Stores Nationwide,” “Across From Columbia Center,” the phone number “783-2499,” and the website “www.crkennewick.com.”
